title: The Role of Communication in the Quality of Relationships for Biological Children in Adoptive Families
abstract: Recent literature reveals the multifaceted experiences of the biologically related children of parents in adoptive families, or adoptive siblings. Adoptive siblings report a range of positive and negative experiences related to adoption. Some adoptive siblings describe their families as fractured or broken after adoption while others describe adoption as bringing their families closer together. Because of the range of experiences expressed, the current study examines specific factors to determine what is related to or affects adoptive sibling's view of their family relationships. Results of this study revealed that no specific family factors were significantly related to adoptive sibling's views; however, adoptive siblings who reported being in families that talked openly and honestly about their thoughts and feelings reported increased family connection and satisfaction and increased sibling closeness with their adopted sibling. These findings elicit further understanding of the adoptive sibling experience and include clinical implications for supporting adoptive families post-adoption.
authors_text: Hunsley, Jana L.; Crawley, Rachel D.; Villaire, Stephanie
